phase_description: Database migration + GDPR scrubbing. Requires human supervision for `supabase db push` and `supabase functions deploy`. Agent writes the files, human runs the deploy commands.
description: Create notifications table with columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (UUID FK to auth.users ON DELETE CASCADE), type (TEXT), data (JSONB DEFAULT '{}'), is_read (BOOLEAN DEFAULT false), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now())
steps_to_verify: Table 'notifications' exists in public schema
Column 'is_read' is used (NOT 'read') — confirmed via \d notifications
user_id has ON DELETE CASCADE referencing auth.users
Inserting a row with missing 'data' defaults to '{}'
Inserting a row with missing 'is_read' defaults to false

description: Create partial index idx_notifications_user_unread on (user_id, created_at DESC) WHERE NOT is_read, and secondary index idx_notifications_user_all on (user_id, created_at DESC)
steps_to_verify: Both indexes exist (query pg_indexes)
EXPLAIN on 'SELECT * FROM notifications WHERE user_id = X AND NOT is_read ORDER BY created_at DESC' uses the partial index

description: Enable RLS on notifications table with two policies: SELECT (own rows), UPDATE (own rows). No INSERT policy (triggers use SECURITY DEFINER). No DELETE policy (users cannot delete notifications — cleanup triggers bypass RLS via SECURITY DEFINER).
steps_to_verify: RLS is enabled on notifications table
Authenticated user can SELECT only their own notifications
Authenticated user can UPDATE (mark as read) only their own notifications
Authenticated user CANNOT insert directly into notifications table
Authenticated user CANNOT delete notifications (no DELETE policy)
Authenticated user CANNOT select/update another user's notifications

description: Create AFTER INSERT trigger on likes table (trg_notify_on_like) that inserts a 'like' notification for the submission owner. Stores actor_id, actor_nickname, actor_avatar, submission_id in data jsonb. Does NOT notify if user liked their own submission. Actor nickname and avatar must be fetched with a single subquery (not two separate ones). All SECURITY DEFINER trigger functions must include SET search_path = public.
steps_to_verify: Liking another user's submission creates a notification with type='like' for the owner
Notification data contains actor_id, actor_nickname, actor_avatar, submission_id
Liking your own submission does NOT create a notification
Trigger function is SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path = public
Actor nickname and avatar are fetched in a single subquery/SELECT INTO, not two separate subqueries

description: Create AFTER INSERT trigger on follows table (trg_notify_on_follow) that inserts a 'follow' notification for the followed user. Includes 24h dedup guard: skips insert if a follow notification from the same actor exists within the last 24 hours. Function must be SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path = public.
steps_to_verify: Following a user creates a notification with type='follow' for the followed user
Notification data contains actor_id, actor_nickname, actor_avatar
Following, unfollowing, then re-following the same user within 24h does NOT create a second follow notification
Following the same user again after 24h DOES create a new notification
Trigger function is SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path = public

description: Create AFTER DELETE trigger on likes table (trg_cleanup_like_notification) that removes the corresponding 'like' notification when a user unlikes a submission. Must scope the DELETE by user_id (the submission owner) so the existing B-tree index is used. Function must be SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path = public.
steps_to_verify: Unliking a submission removes the 'like' notification matching that actor_id + submission_id
Other users' like notifications for the same submission are NOT affected
DELETE query includes user_id in the WHERE clause (look up submission owner) for index usage
Trigger function is SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path = public

description: Create AFTER DELETE trigger on follows table (trg_cleanup_follow_notification) that removes the corresponding 'follow' notification when a user unfollows. Function must be SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path = public.
steps_to_verify: Unfollowing a user removes the 'follow' notification matching that actor_id for the unfollowed user
Other follow notifications for the same user are NOT affected
Trigger function is SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path = public

description: Create AFTER INSERT trigger on submissions table (trg_notify_on_submission) that inserts a 'friend_submitted' notification for each follower. Wrapped in EXCEPTION block so notification failures never roll back the submission INSERT. Includes NOT EXISTS dedup guard per follower + submission. Function must be SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path = public.
steps_to_verify: Submitting artwork creates 'friend_submitted' notifications for all followers of the submitter
Each notification contains actor_id, actor_nickname, actor_avatar, submission_id
If notification insertion fails (e.g. simulated error), the submission INSERT still succeeds
Duplicate friend_submitted notifications for the same follower+submission are prevented
Trigger function is SECURITY DEFINER with SET search_path = public

description: Add notifications table to Supabase Realtime publication (ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE notifications)
steps_to_verify: notifications table is listed in supabase_realtime publication

description: Add pg_cron job to the notifications migration that deletes notifications older than 30 days, running daily at 03:00 UTC. Uses pg_cron (available on Pro plan) — no edge function needed.
steps_to_verify: pg_cron job 'cleanup-notifications' is scheduled in the migration SQL
Cron schedule is '0 3 * * *' (daily at 03:00 UTC)
Job runs: DELETE FROM notifications WHERE created_at < now() - interval '30 days'
No cleanup-notifications edge function exists (not needed with pg_cron)

description: Migration file created at supabase/migrations/20260407000001_notifications.sql containing all of the above (table, indexes, RLS, triggers, realtime, pg_cron schedule, scrub_notification_actor function). Human runs `supabase db push` to deploy.
steps_to_verify: File exists at supabase/migrations/20260408000004_notifications.sql
Migration includes pg_cron schedule for cleanup-notifications
Migration includes scrub_notification_actor function for GDPR scrubbing
Migration runs successfully via supabase db push with no errors

description: Add GDPR notification scrubbing to the existing delete-account edge function (supabase/functions/delete-account/index.ts). Before deleting the auth user, call scrub_notification_actor RPC to strip actor_nickname and actor_avatar from all notification rows where data->>'actor_id' matches the user's id. Human runs `supabase functions deploy delete-account` to deploy.
steps_to_verify: delete-account edge function calls scrub_notification_actor RPC BEFORE the auth user delete
After account deletion, other users' notifications referencing the deleted user display 'Someone' instead of the old name
No broken avatar URLs remain in notification data for deleted users
UI falls back to 'Someone' when actor_nickname is null or missing in notification data

phase_description: Frontend code for poll-based notifications (Phase 1). All local repo changes — no external deploys. Agent can run tsc and eslint to verify.
description: Create src/types/notifications.ts with NotificationType union ('like' | 'follow' | 'friend_submitted'), NotificationDataMap interface with per-type data shapes (including actor_nickname, actor_avatar where applicable), and a Notification discriminated union type using mapped types so n.type narrows n.data automatically.
steps_to_verify: File exists at src/types/notifications.ts
NotificationType does NOT include 'competition_result'
In a switch on n.type, n.data is correctly narrowed (e.g. case 'like' gives access to n.data.submission_id without casting)
Notification uses is_read (not read)
No 'as Record<string, unknown>' casts needed when consuming Notification in switch statements
tsc --noEmit passes with no errors

description: Create src/config/notificationIcons.ts exporting NOTIFICATION_ICONS — a Record<NotificationType, { icon, bgClass, colorClass }> mapping each notification type to its Lucide icon and Tailwind color classes. Used by both NotificationsTab and ToastContainer.
steps_to_verify: File exists at src/config/notificationIcons.ts
Exports NOTIFICATION_ICONS with entries for: like (Heart), follow (UserPlus), friend_submitted (Store)
Does NOT include competition_result or milestone
Does NOT import unused icons (e.g. Star from lucide-react — competition_result was removed)
Both NotificationsTab and ToastContainer import from this file (no duplicated icon maps)

description: Add fetchNotifications(userId) to src/lib/api.ts — fetches most recent 50 notifications with submission data joined via FK, ordered by created_at DESC. No separate thumbnail fetch needed.
steps_to_verify: Function exists in src/lib/api.ts
Returns Notification[] with submissions field populated via PostgREST join
Orders by created_at descending
Limits to 50 results
No fetchSubmissionThumbnailsBatch function exists (removed)

description: Add fetchUnreadCount(userId) to src/lib/api.ts — returns count of unread notifications using head:true for efficiency
steps_to_verify: Function exists and returns a number
Uses { count: 'exact', head: true } for efficiency (no row data transferred)
Filters on is_read = false

description: Add markNotificationRead(notificationId) and markAllNotificationsRead(userId) to src/lib/api.ts. No deleteNotification — users cannot delete notifications.
steps_to_verify: markNotificationRead sets is_read=true for a single notification
markAllNotificationsRead sets is_read=true for all unread notifications of a user
No deleteNotification function exists
Both functions use is_read (not read)

description: Create src/hooks/notifications/useNotifications.ts — manages notification state (fetch all 50 on mount, no pagination), optimistic mark-as-read, error state, and a prependNotification method for Realtime (Phase 2). unreadCount must use fetchUnreadCount for accurate server-side count. prependNotification must deduplicate by id before prepending.
steps_to_verify: Hook returns: notifications, unreadCount, loading, fetched, error, markRead, markAllRead, prependNotification, reload
Load fetches 50 notifications on mount (no pagination, no load-more)
markRead performs optimistic update (is_read=true immediately) and rolls back on failure
markAllRead performs optimistic update and reloads from server on failure
On fetch error: error state is set, fetched is set to true (prevents infinite retry loop), loading is false
error state is cleared on successful fetch
fetchNotifications is imported (simple fetch, no pagination)
unreadCount uses fetchUnreadCount (API-002) for accurate server-side count, not notifications.filter(n => !n.is_read).length
prependNotification checks for existing id before prepending (dedup guard for Realtime reconnection duplicates)

description: Create src/contexts/NotificationsContext.tsx — wraps useNotifications in a React context. Provider accepts userId prop. useNotificationsContext() hook throws if used outside provider.
steps_to_verify: NotificationsProvider component exists and accepts userId + children
useNotificationsContext hook exists and throws descriptive error if used outside provider
Context value includes error field
Provider is mounted in a parent that wraps both UserMenuDropdown trigger and panel content

description: Create src/components/notifications/NotificationsTab.tsx — renders notification list inside UserMenuDropdown as the 'Notifications' tab content. Uses type-safe discriminated union (no Record<string, unknown> casts). Shows loading state, error state with retry button, and empty state.
steps_to_verify: Component renders a scrollable list of notifications with viewport-relative max-height (e.g. max-h-[40vh], NOT fixed 300px — must scale with viewport)
Each notification shows: type icon (from shared NOTIFICATION_ICONS), text with actor name (bold), relative timestamp
getNotificationText uses type narrowing (switch on n.type) — no 'as Record<string, unknown>' casts anywhere in the file
Actor names fall back to 'Someone' when null/missing
Loading state shows 'Loading...' when loading with no existing notifications
Error state shows 'Failed to load notifications.' with a clickable 'Retry' button
Empty state shows helpful copy (e.g. 'When someone likes your art or follows you, you'll see it here.') — not just 'No notifications yet'
'Mark all as read' button appears in header only when there are unread notifications

description: NotificationItem hover-to-read: hovering for 700ms marks notification as read. CSS progress bar on left edge animates over 700ms in sync. Mouse leave cancels both timer and animation.
steps_to_verify: Hovering over an unread notification for 700ms marks it as read (optimistic — instant visual feedback)
A 3px accent-colored progress bar animates top-to-bottom on the left edge during hover
Moving mouse away before 700ms cancels the timer — notification stays unread
Moving mouse away resets the progress bar animation
Already-read notifications do not start the timer on hover
@keyframes fillBar is defined in index.css

description: NotificationItem keyboard accessibility: focusing a notification via Tab triggers the same 700ms mark-as-read behavior as hover. Blur cancels it.
steps_to_verify: Each NotificationItem has tabIndex={0}
Tabbing to an unread notification starts the 700ms read timer (onFocus)
Tabbing away cancels the timer (onBlur)
Pressing Enter/clicking a focused notification marks it as read and navigates

description: NotificationItem click behavior: clicking marks as read (if unread) and navigates to the relevant target. Like/friend_submitted → submission view. Follow → profile view. Navigation closes the dropdown.
steps_to_verify: Clicking a 'like' notification navigates to ?view=submission&id=<submission_id>
Clicking a 'follow' notification navigates to ?view=profile&user=<actor_id>
Clicking a 'friend_submitted' notification navigates to ?view=submission&id=<submission_id>
Click marks notification as read if it was unread
Click closes the UserMenuDropdown (onClose is called)

description: NotificationItem visual states: unread items use bg-(--color-accent-subtle) (pink accent tint), read items use bg-(--color-card-bg) (white/card). Read items hover to bg-(--color-hover). Icon containers are 32px rounded squares (rounded-lg) with type-specific colored backgrounds. Submission thumbnails rendered from joined submission data + baked-in colors (no extra API calls).
steps_to_verify: Unread notifications have visibly different background from read notifications
Read notifications use --color-card-bg background
Unread notifications use --color-accent-subtle background
Icon containers are rounded squares (rounded-lg / 0.5rem), not circles
Like/friend_submitted notifications show submission thumbnail (36px) on right side via FK join
Follow notifications show no thumbnail
No separate thumbnail fetch effect in NotificationsTab — data comes from fetchNotifications join

description: Extend UserMenuDropdown with Notifications as the first tab. Tab order: Notifications | Following | Followers. Default to Notifications tab if unread items exist, otherwise Following.
steps_to_verify: Three tabs visible in UserMenuDropdown: Notifications, Following, Followers
Tab labels show counts in smaller text when > 0, e.g. 'Notifications (3)', 'Following (8)'. Counts hidden when 0.
Notifications is the first tab
When there are unread notifications, dropdown opens to Notifications tab
When there are no unread notifications, dropdown opens to Following tab
Log Out button remains visible regardless of active tab
Switching between tabs works correctly

description: Show unread notification dot on the avatar button in the top bar. Dot is a 10px (w-2.5 h-2.5) accent-colored circle with 2px border matching button background, positioned at top-right of avatar.
steps_to_verify: Dot appears on avatar when unreadCount > 0
Dot disappears when all notifications are read
Dot is accent color with 2px border matching --color-bg-secondary
Dot has aria-label with unread count for screen readers
Dot is positioned at top-right corner of the avatar (-top-0.5 -right-0.5)

description: NotificationsProvider must be mounted in a parent above UserMenuDropdown so unread count is available to both the trigger button (for dot) and the panel content.
steps_to_verify: NotificationsProvider wraps the component tree containing UserMenuDropdown
NotificationsProvider is NOT mounted inside UserMenuDropdown itself
userId is passed from the existing auth context

description: Add @keyframes fillBar (scaleY 0->1, opacity 0->0.6 over 700ms) to index.css
steps_to_verify: @keyframes fillBar is defined in index.css with from: scaleY(0) opacity 0, to: scaleY(1) opacity 0.6

description: Migration to add submission_id FK column to notifications table, backfill from JSONB data, bake challenge colors into notification JSONB, and update notify_on_like + notify_on_submission triggers to populate both. Human runs `supabase db push` to deploy.
steps_to_verify: submission_id column exists on notifications table as UUID REFERENCES submissions(id)
Existing notifications have submission_id backfilled from data->>'submission_id'
Existing notifications with submissions have colors array in JSONB data
New like notifications populate submission_id column and data.colors
New friend_submitted notifications populate submission_id column and data.colors
fetchNotifications joins on submissions via FK — no separate thumbnail fetch needed
Migration runs successfully via supabase db push with no errors

phase_description: Realtime subscription + toast system (Phase 2). All local repo changes — no external deploys. DB already has Realtime enabled from Phase 0.
description: Create src/contexts/ToastContext.tsx — manages toast state with addToast, dismissToast, max 4 toasts (oldest evicted when exceeded)
steps_to_verify: ToastProvider exists and provides toasts, addToast, dismissToast
Max 4 toasts displayed at once — oldest is evicted when a 5th is added
dismissToast removes a toast by id
useToast hook throws if used outside provider

description: Create src/components/notifications/ToastContainer.tsx — renders toasts in bottom-right corner. Each toast shows icon (from shared NOTIFICATION_ICONS), text, action link. Uses framer-motion for slide-in/out animations.
steps_to_verify: Toasts render in fixed position at bottom-5 right-5, z-index 200
Each toast shows the correct icon and colors from shared NOTIFICATION_ICONS
Toast text uses type narrowing (no Record<string, unknown> casts)
Toast shows actor name (falls back to 'Someone') and action text (e.g. 'View submission ->')
Slide-in animation from right (x: 32 -> 0) with spring physics
Exit animation slides back right with opacity fade
Toasts stack newest-on-top (flex-col-reverse)
AnimatePresence mode='popLayout' handles smooth reflow on dismiss

description: Toast auto-dismiss: 5s timer with progress bar that drains left-to-right. Hovering pauses both timer and progress bar. Close button appears on hover. CRITICAL: the JS dismiss timer and CSS progress bar must use a single source of truth for elapsed time — track remaining time on pause and resume from that value (do NOT restart a fresh 5s timer on unpause, or the bar and timer will desync).
steps_to_verify: Toast auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
2px accent-colored progress bar at bottom drains from 100% to 0% over 5s
Hovering pauses the 5s timer and progress bar animation
Unhovering resumes both timer and progress bar FROM WHERE THEY LEFT OFF (not from scratch)
Timer tracks remaining time on pause — e.g. pausing at 2s means 3s remaining on resume
Progress bar and JS timer stay in sync after pause/resume cycles
Close button (x) appears on hover at top-right
Clicking close button dismisses the toast (event.stopPropagation prevents navigation)
@keyframes progressDrain is defined in index.css

description: Clicking a toast navigates to the relevant target (same routing as panel notifications) and dismisses the toast
steps_to_verify: Clicking a 'like' toast navigates to ?view=submission&id=<submission_id>
Clicking a 'follow' toast navigates to ?view=profile&user=<actor_id>
Clicking a 'friend_submitted' toast navigates to ?view=submission&id=<submission_id>
Toast is dismissed after click

description: Create src/hooks/notifications/useNotificationsRealtime.ts — subscribes to Supabase Realtime INSERT events on notifications table filtered by user_id. On new notification: prepends to notification list and fires a toast.
steps_to_verify: Hook subscribes to postgres_changes INSERT on notifications table
Filter is user_id=eq.${userId} (server-side filtering)
New notification is prepended to the in-memory list via prependNotification
New notification triggers a toast via addToast
Subscription cleans up on unmount (supabase.removeChannel)
Hook does nothing when userId is null

description: useNotificationsRealtime is mounted in the app root alongside NotificationsProvider. Toasts only fire for Realtime events, not on initial load.
steps_to_verify: Hook is called in the same component that renders NotificationsProvider
Initial page load does NOT show toasts for existing notifications
A new like/follow/submission from another user triggers a toast in real time

description: On Realtime channel reconnection, call reload() to catch any notifications missed during the disconnect gap. Supabase Realtime does not replay missed events.
steps_to_verify: When Realtime subscription reconnects after a disconnect, reload() is called to fetch missed notifications
No duplicate toasts fire for notifications that arrived during the disconnect (they were fetched by reload, not Realtime)

description: Add @keyframes progressDrain (width 100%->0%) to index.css
steps_to_verify: @keyframes progressDrain is defined in index.css with from: width 100%, to: width 0%

phase_description: Browser testing with two accounts. Requires dev server running and human present to verify visual results.
description: Full Phase 1 integration: User A likes User B's submission -> User B sees 'like' notification in panel with A's name, correct icon, relative timestamp. Clicking navigates to submission.
steps_to_verify: Like from User A creates notification visible in User B's panel
Notification shows User A's nickname in bold
Heart icon with accent color is displayed
Relative timestamp is shown (e.g. 'just now', '5m ago')
Clicking navigates to the submission view
Unread dot appears on User B's avatar
After marking as read, dot disappears (if no other unreads)

description: Full Phase 1 integration: User A follows User B -> User B sees 'follow' notification. User A unfollows -> notification is removed.
steps_to_verify: Follow creates notification with UserPlus icon and A's name
Clicking navigates to User A's profile
Unfollowing removes the notification from User B's panel

description: Full Phase 1 integration: User A (followed by User B) submits artwork -> User B sees 'friend_submitted' notification.
steps_to_verify: Submission by User A creates notification for follower User B
Notification shows Store icon and A's name
Clicking navigates to the submission view
User A's submission was saved successfully (not rolled back by notification system)

description: Full Phase 2 integration: User A likes User B's submission while User B has the app open -> toast appears in bottom-right with slide-in animation, auto-dismisses after 5s.
steps_to_verify: Toast appears in real time without page refresh
Toast slides in from right with spring animation
Toast shows Heart icon, User A's name, 'View submission ->' action
Toast auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
Notification also appears in the panel (prepended to list)

description: Move features/current-feature/plan-notifications.md to docs/plan-notifications.md (the features/ directory is ephemeral and may be deleted). Add a brief mention in the project README linking to docs/plan-notifications.md as the authoritative documentation for the notifications system.
steps_to_verify: docs/plan-notifications.md exists with the full plan content
features/current-feature/plan-notifications.md no longer exists (moved, not copied)
README mentions the notifications system with a link to docs/plan-notifications.md
